steps.title: Rewrite the given equation in slope form
Test all four choices for parallel (no solution) vs. other outcomes
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$x = 5y$': Rearranging gives $y = rac{1}{5}x$. Slope $= rac{1}{5} eq rac{1}{15}$. Different slope → lines intersect → one solution. ✗
  • Choice B — '$ rac{1}{3}x = 5y$': Multiply both sides by 3: $x = 15y$, so $y = rac{1}{15}x$. Slope $= rac{1}{15}$ (same), $y$-intercept $= 0 eq rac{3}{5}$ (different). Parallel lines → no solution. ✓
  • Choice C — '$x = 15y - 9$': Gives $y = rac{1}{15}x + rac{3}{5}$. Same slope AND same intercept as the given equation → identical lines → infinitely many solutions, not no solution. ✗
  • Choice D — '$ rac{1}{3}x = 15y - 9$': Multiply by 3: $x = 45y - 27$, so $y = rac{1}{45}x + rac{3}{5}$. Slope $= rac{1}{45} eq rac{1}{15}$ (the original). Different slope → lines intersect at one point → exactly one solution. ✗

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: Only Choice B produces parallel lines (same slope, different $y$-intercept), which means no solution. That matches choice B — B is correct.
